source_prompt: 그럼 구현 난이도,, 어떻게 구현을 해야할지, 판단을 해보자, 어떻게 워크 플로우를 짜야, 제대로 구현을 다 할까,, 비판 리뷰까지 판단해서

activation.reason: The objective spans architecture, CLI contracts, candidate rendering, sandbox security, semantic scene modeling, composition lint, MCP surfaces, tests, visual dogfood, and adversarial review. It needs resumable phase artifacts and human gates before any source/apply/release path.

objective: Implement the LLM-amplifying composition search engine in figure-agent without suppressing LLM creativity or bypassing the quality kernel: host-authored LLM proposals enter as structured candidates, render in sandboxes, pass freshness/invariant/sandbox/composition checks, produce comparison packets, and require human acceptance before fixture mutation.

assumptions.claim: Existing candidate_render/candidate_apply infrastructure can be extended instead of replaced.
assumptions.verification: Inspect candidate_render.py and candidate_apply.py for sandbox/path validation, manifest schemas, and render/apply lifecycles before writing new modules. If extension would create unsafe compatibility shims, create composition_* modules that reuse only runtime_paths and artifact helpers.

assumptions.claim: v1 must not call model APIs from plugin code.
assumptions.verification: Search final diff for network/model/API clients and for commands that generate candidates from --goal text. compose-capture must consume proposal JSON only.

assumptions.claim: fig3_resistance_mechanism is a valid dogfood target but currently stale.
assumptions.verification: Run bin/fig-agent status fig3_resistance_mechanism from plugin root; first dogfood wave must refresh compile/critique/export or record proposed_unranked only.

assumptions.claim: Composition lint can be deterministic enough for v1.
assumptions.verification: Each lint check must declare metric/evidence/threshold; any check lacking these is emitted as human_commentary and cannot rank or block.

phases.title: Contract scaffold and RED tests
phases.exit: Schema fixtures and failing tests exist for freshness-vector, compose-capture, semantic invariant coverage, candidate operation payload, acceptance hash binding, and sandbox escape rejection.
tasks: Add schema validation helpers or fixture JSON examples for composition-candidate-set, freshness-vector, semantic-invariant, composition-lint-check, composition-candidate-operation, composition-acceptance.

Write RED tests that reject natural-language-only proposals, plugin-side --goal generation, missing operation payload, stale ranking, missing acceptance hashes, and path/symlink/shell-escape sandbox attempts.
Record current fig3 status and stale evidence as baseline.

phases.title: Freshness state machine and compose-capture
phases.exit: compose-capture stores host-authored proposal JSON as proposed_unranked even with stale detector evidence, while sandbox_prepare returns rebase_required on source hash drift.
tasks: Implement freshness-vector computation and invalidation helpers.
Implement compose-capture CLI with proposal JSON validation and no model calls.
Add tests for stale proposal capture vs stale rank/apply refusal.

phases.title: TeX sandbox runner hardening
phases.exit: compose-render can render safe candidate source copies and rejects absolute paths, ../ escapes, symlinks, forbidden input roots, write escapes, and shell-escape attempts.
tasks: Extend or wrap candidate_render.py with composition sandbox runner.
Canonicalize copied inputs and outputs before and immediately before compile.
Run TeX with isolated cwd/home/temp roots and shell escape disabled.
Record copied asset hashes and denied-path diagnostics.

phases.title: Semantic blocks and scene model v1
phases.exit: fig3 scene model can address carrier_walk, current_sparkline, bridge_text, distribution curves, n_breadth, rho60s, and candidate operations can target semantic blocks instead of raw line ranges.
tasks: Define semantic block marker convention and parser.
Add scene model builder from explicit fixture annotations plus source markers.
Add invariant coverage report for target objects.
Prepare fig3-only annotations without applying visual redraws yet.

phases.title: Composition lint v1
phases.exit: Composition lint emits deterministic or human_commentary findings for orphan_plot, floating_annotation, arrow_clutter, measurement_arrow_crosses_data, thin_glitch_primitive, panel_density_imbalance, path_mechanicalness, and anchor_ambiguity.
tasks: Implement lint check registry with required metric/evidence/threshold metadata.
Demote unmeasurable checks to human_commentary.
Produce fig3 lint JSON and tests for deterministic checks.

phases.title: Creative candidate render/rank/review
phases.entry: P1 + P2 + P3 complete; P4 at least arrow_clutter and orphan_plot ready
phases.exit: compose-render, compose-rank, and compose-review produce before/after artifacts and metric-backed review packets for at least one host-authored fig3 proposal.
tasks: Implement compose-render and candidate review packet generation. First shipped slice is prepare-only: source-copy artifacts and review packets are emitted without TeX execution.
Implement compose-rank using hard gates plus declared deterministic lint deltas only.
Support freeform_structural as review-only and never auto-apply.
Add MCP capture/render/rank/review handlers after CLI contracts pass in a later slice.

phases.title: Acceptance and apply gate
phases.exit: Human acceptance artifact can authorize applying a typed patch only when hashes, invariant coverage, sandbox render, and hard gates still match; full_source_copy remains render-only unless converted.
tasks: Implement composition-acceptance validation and hash binding.
Implement dry-run apply for typed semantic-block replacement.
Require explicit permissions for accept_freeform_structural and change_truth_bearing_geometry.
Rerun hard gates immediately before mutation.

phases.title: Fig3 dogfood, judge panel, and adversarial review
phases.exit: At least three fig3 creative candidates render, rank, and compare; independent reviewers find no P0/P1 contract bypass; human can choose a candidate or reject all with evidence preserved.
tasks: Host LLM authors three proposal JSONs: carrier walk rewrite, sparkline anchor, arrow simplification.
Run compose-capture/render/rank/review for all three.
Run judge panel: visual composition, scientific correctness, security/sandbox, reproducibility.
Patch spec or code until adversarial review has no P0/P1 findings or stop with documented blockers.

workers.role: Own schemas, state transitions, CLI/MCP boundary, and no-model-call invariant.
forbidden_actions: call model APIs from plugin code

workers.role: Own TeX sandbox runner and hostile candidate tests.

must_exclude: MCP handlers unless needed after CLI passes

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: diff + pass/fail matrix for sandbox attacks
stop_conditions: safe candidates render and hostile candidates fail
TeX confinement cannot be enforced -> stop with risk report

workers.role: Own semantic scene model, composition lint, creative candidate review packets.

prompt_contract.required_output_schema: scene JSON + lint JSON + review packet JSON + test log
stop_conditions: needs human taste choice -> stop at gate

workers.role: Independent verifier trying to refute safety, no-bypass, and no-fake-taste claims.
forbidden_actions: trust producer claims without reading outputs

workers.role: User or delegated human authority for accepting creative candidates and truth-bearing changes.

handoffs.artifact: captured proposal candidate set with freshness-vector
accepted or rejected composition candidate decision

parallelism.fan_in_rule: P5 is the main fan-in barrier: it requires P1 freshness/capture, P2 sandbox render, P3 scene model, and at least the P4 deterministic lint subset. P6 requires P5. P7 runs after P5, with P6 dry-run optional unless the user wants apply.

verification.claim_or_output: The plugin does not suppress LLM proposals and does not bypass itself.
verification.falsifier: A proposal JSON outside known families is captured as proposed_unranked, but no ranking/apply occurs until freshness and executable payload checks pass.
verification.evidence_required: compose-capture output + rejected compose-rank on stale evidence + accepted freeform_structural render-only packet

verification.claim_or_output: The plugin never performs plugin-side LLM generation in v1.
verification.falsifier: Search diff and command behavior for model/API calls or generation from --goal text; none exist.
verification.evidence_required: rg transcript and CLI help showing proposal-json capture path

verification.claim_or_output: TeX sandbox blocks hostile candidate input.
verification.falsifier: Absolute path, ../ escape, symlink, forbidden input, write escape, and shell-escape fixtures all fail with explicit diagnostics.
verification.evidence_required: pytest matrix from sandbox negative tests

verification.claim_or_output: Composition lint does not become fake taste oracle.
verification.falsifier: Unmeasurable checks are emitted as human_commentary and excluded from rank/block decisions; deterministic checks cite metrics and evidence hashes.
verification.evidence_required: composition lint JSON and compose-rank JSON

verification.claim_or_output: fig3 dogfood demonstrates value beyond micro-nudge.
verification.falsifier: At least three structural candidates render; at least one changes topology or object anchoring; review packet shows before/after and metric-backed deltas.
verification.evidence_required: candidate manifests, rendered before/after artifacts, review packet, human decision

risk_gates.trigger: Any command applies a creative candidate to fixture source.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do not apply. Stop at review packet unless a composition-acceptance.v1 artifact grants the required permissions and all accepted hashes still match.

risk_gates.trigger: A candidate changes truth-bearing geometry or scientific metaphor.
risk_gates.safe_default: Render and review only. Require explicit human waiver or permission change_truth_bearing_geometry before apply.

risk_gates.trigger: Adding dependencies or changing TeX engine/sandbox policy.
risk_gates.safe_default: Do not add dependencies. First attempt with stdlib/path/env isolation and current lualatex; escalate if insufficient.

risk_gates.trigger: Network, paid API, or plugin-originated model call proposed.
risk_gates.safe_default: Reject for v1; use host-authored proposal JSON instead.

risk_gates.trigger: Export, accepted, golden, or release-ready state mutation.
risk_gates.safe_default: Out of scope for implementation waves; require separate explicit user approval.
Do not commit or push without explicit user instruction.

budget.time_budget: multi-session; 1-2 days for P0-P2, 2-4 days for P3-P5, additional 1-2 days for P6-P7 hardening

resume.invalidation: Spec edits invalidate P0 and downstream; candidate source/spec/briefing changes invalidate P5+; detector/reference changes invalidate rank/review but not proposal capture; sandbox policy edits invalidate P2 and all rendered artifacts.

first_slice.instruction: Start with P0 only: add schema examples and RED tests for composition contracts, freshness-vector, proposal capture, executable operation payloads, acceptance hash binding, and sandbox attack rejection. Do not implement composition rendering yet.
first_slice.expected_output: New or extended tests that fail for the current implementation for the right reasons, plus JSON schema fixtures under tests/fixtures or examples/<name>/build/candidates test sandbox.
first_slice.completion_check: uv run pytest tests/test_composition_contracts.py tests/test_candidate_render.py -q shows the new tests failing before implementation or passing only after a focused P0 implementation; no source fixture mutation.

slices.instruction: Write contract/schema RED tests and JSON fixtures.
slices.expected_output: Failing tests for missing composition contract implementation.
slices.completion_check: New tests fail for intended missing features, not import/path mistakes.

slices.instruction: Write hostile TeX sandbox negative tests.
slices.expected_output: Hostile fixtures covering abs path, ../, symlink, forbidden input, write escape, shell escape.
slices.completion_check: Tests currently fail or are marked xfail with exact missing policy codes.

first_wave.entry_gate: Spec file present and reviewed; user agrees to design-first P0/P2 test slice.
first_wave.exit_gate: All RED tests have clear failure reasons and no source fixture mutation occurred.
first_wave.fan_in: Contract architect reviews both test sets for schema consistency.

waves.entry_gate: Wave 1 RED tests accepted as valid.
waves.exit_gate: compose-capture and compose-render pass their contract and sandbox tests.
fig3 scene model and lint reports are deterministic and review-only where metrics are absent.

waves.entry_gate: fig3 has scene+lint and at least one host proposal JSON.
waves.exit_gate: Three fig3 creative candidates render/rank/review; adversarial review reports no P0/P1; human gate packet is ready.
